claims.claim_text: Cilium and Hubble can show dropped traffic and the reason it was dropped, including policy-related violations.
claims.span_hint: section: The 3 AM Wake-Up Call
claims.rationale: The Cilium repository README directly describes flow visibility, drop reasons, and policy-related troubleshooting.

claims.claim_text: Unsourced war story about a 127-service microservices architecture and a mysterious health-check failure.
claims.span_hint: section: Why This Module Matters
claims.anchor_text: We had a microservices architecture—127 services, because apparently we thought Netflix was a good role model. One service was mysteriously failing health checks. The app worked fine when tested directly. Network team said the network was fine. App team said the app was fine. Classic standoff.
claims.suggested_rewrite: We had a microservices architecture with enough moving parts that a network-policy mistake was hard to isolate. One service was failing health checks even though direct application tests passed, and each team initially believed its own layer was fine.
claims.rationale: This is a specific unattributed incident with a precise service count and should be rewritten to preserve the lesson without fake specifics.

claims.claim_text: Unsourced claim that a legacy policy written 18 months earlier by a departed engineer blocked traffic.
claims.span_hint: section: Why This Module Matters
claims.anchor_text: **The legacy-lockdown policy.** Written 18 months ago by someone who left the company. Blocked traffic from a service that didn't exist when the policy was created.
claims.suggested_rewrite: **An older policy was the culprit.** It had outlived the assumptions it was originally written for and blocked a dependency the team had overlooked.
claims.rationale: This is precise historical detail about a real-sounding incident with no source.

claims.claim_text: Unsourced claim that the issue was a five-minute fix and would otherwise have taken far longer.
claims.span_hint: section: Why This Module Matters
claims.anchor_text: Five-minute fix. Without Cilium, we'd still be debugging.
claims.suggested_rewrite: The root cause was visible quickly, turning a long network investigation into a straightforward policy fix.
claims.rationale: The exact duration is uncited false precision in a war story.

claims.claim_text: Blockquote claims Google chose eBPF for next-generation internal networking, that iptables updates took minutes while eBPF updates took microseconds, and that GKE, EKS, and AKS now all offer Cilium as their CNI.
claims.span_hint: section: Why This Module Matters / Did You Know
claims.anchor_text: > 💡 **Did You Know?** When Google designed their next-generation internal networking, they chose eBPF—the same technology powering Cilium. The reason? At Google scale, traditional iptables rules would take *minutes* to update. With eBPF, updates happen in microseconds. Google Cloud GKE, AWS EKS, and Azure AKS all now offer Cilium as their CNI. It's not just an alternative anymore—it's becoming the default.
claims.suggested_rewrite: > 💡 **Did You Know?** eBPF-based dataplanes are attractive in Kubernetes because they reduce dependence on large iptables rule sets and enable richer observability.
claims.rationale: This bundles multiple unsupported or overstated specifics, including vendor-positioning claims that are not accurate as written.

claims.claim_text: Large clusters can end up with 147,000 lines of iptables rules.
claims.span_hint: section: The IPTables Nightmare
claims.anchor_text: **One hundred and forty-seven thousand lines of iptables rules.**
claims.suggested_rewrite: **Very large iptables rule sets are common in bigger clusters.**
claims.rationale: The exact number is illustrative and not sourced to a primary reference.

claims.claim_text: Large-scale Kubernetes users universally agree that iptables does not scale.
claims.span_hint: section: The IPTables Nightmare
claims.anchor_text: Large-scale Kubernetes users universally agree: iptables doesn't scale.
claims.suggested_rewrite: At larger cluster sizes, iptables-based service routing can become a real operational bottleneck.
claims.rationale: The universal claim is stronger than the cited evidence and should be narrowed.

claims.claim_text: The `cilium connectivity test` command deploys test workloads and runs multiple connectivity checks, with optional Hubble flow validation.
claims.span_hint: section: Installation: Your First Cilium Cluster
claims.rationale: The Cilium CLI repository directly shows connectivity validation and optional flow validation with Hubble.

claims.claim_text: Cilium uses an identity-based security model derived from labels rather than IP addresses, and endpoints sharing the same relevant labels share an identity.
claims.span_hint: section: How Cilium Identity Works
claims.rationale: The Cilium repository README directly explains the identity-based model and label-derived identities.

claims.claim_text: Cilium reserves identity numbers 1-255, with identity 1 as host, 2 as world, and 4 as health.
claims.span_hint: section: Seeing Identities in Action / Did You Know
claims.rationale: This exact numeric mapping is documented in official Cilium docs, but that domain is off the current allowlist.

claims.claim_text: Cilium can enforce L7-aware policies based on HTTP method, path, and headers.
claims.span_hint: section: CiliumNetworkPolicy - The Enhanced Version
claims.rationale: The Cilium repository README explicitly describes L7-aware policy filtering by HTTP method, path, and headers.

claims.claim_text: Cilium policy entities include host, remote-node, kube-apiserver, health, dns, and world, each with defined semantics.
claims.span_hint: section: The power of `toEntities`
claims.rationale: The exact entity semantics are documented in official Cilium policy language docs, which are off-allowlist.

claims.claim_text: Hubble Relay provides cluster-wide visibility and Hubble UI provides a service-map style visualization layer.
claims.span_hint: section: Cilium Architecture - The Big Picture
claims.rationale: Official Hubble docs describe node-local scope, Relay for cluster scope, and UI for visualization, but the docs host is off-allowlist.

claims.claim_text: Hubble sees every packet and does not rely on sampling.
claims.span_hint: section: Hubble Metrics for Prometheus / Did You Know
claims.anchor_text: Unlike traditional monitoring that might capture 1 in 1000 packets, Hubble sees EVERY packet. If something happened on the network, Hubble saw it.
claims.suggested_rewrite: Compared with coarse network monitoring, Hubble gives detailed flow-level visibility that is especially useful for troubleshooting and auditing.
claims.rationale: The absolute no-sampling/every-packet claim is too strong without a precise primary source and depends on deployment details.

claims.claim_text: Service lookup latency is ~2ms with kube-proxy iptables at 5000 services versus ~100μs with Cilium eBPF, a 20x improvement.
claims.span_hint: section: Performance Comparison table
claims.anchor_text: | Service lookup latency | ~2ms (5000 services) | ~100μs | **20x faster** |
claims.suggested_rewrite: | Service lookup latency | Can increase as iptables rule sets grow | Often lower with eBPF-based service handling | Context-dependent |
claims.rationale: This is a precise benchmark row with no attributable primary benchmark source.

claims.claim_text: Memory usage grows with services for kube-proxy iptables and stays constant with Cilium eBPF.
claims.span_hint: section: Performance Comparison table
claims.anchor_text: | Memory usage | Grows with services | Constant | **Predictable** |
claims.suggested_rewrite: | Memory usage | Often grows with rule-set size | Often more predictable with eBPF maps | Workload-dependent |
claims.rationale: The row makes an absolute performance claim without a cited benchmark or methodology.

claims.claim_text: Rule update time is 5-30 seconds with kube-proxy iptables and milliseconds with Cilium eBPF, a 1000x improvement.
claims.span_hint: section: Performance Comparison table
claims.anchor_text: | Rule update time | 5-30 seconds | Milliseconds | **1000x faster** |
claims.suggested_rewrite: | Rule update time | Can slow down noticeably on large rule sets | Usually faster with eBPF-based updates | Environment-dependent |
claims.rationale: This is an exact benchmark comparison with no cited primary source.

claims.claim_text: Connection drops happen on update with kube-proxy iptables but not with Cilium eBPF.
claims.span_hint: section: Performance Comparison table
claims.anchor_text: | Connection drops on update | Yes | No | **Zero downtime** |
claims.suggested_rewrite: | Connection drops on update | More likely during disruptive rule churn | Typically reduced with eBPF-based updates | Depends on configuration and rollout path |
claims.rationale: The absolute yes/no and zero-downtime framing is stronger than can be supported without a specific benchmark and environment.

claims.claim_text: CPU usage is reduced by 50-70% at scale with Cilium eBPF compared with kube-proxy iptables.
claims.span_hint: section: Performance Comparison table
claims.anchor_text: | CPU usage at scale | High | Low | **50-70% reduction** |
claims.suggested_rewrite: | CPU usage at scale | Can rise with service and rule volume | Can be lower with eBPF-based handling | Depends on traffic and cluster shape |
claims.rationale: This is a specific percentage claim with no primary benchmark source.

claims.claim_text: Cilium supports transparent WireGuard encryption for traffic between Cilium-managed endpoints.
claims.span_hint: section: Transparent Encryption with WireGuard
claims.rationale: The official Cilium WireGuard guide directly documents this capability, but the docs host is off-allowlist.

claims.claim_text: Hubble exports Prometheus metrics under the `hubble_` namespace, including DNS, drop, and HTTP metrics.
claims.span_hint: section: Hubble Metrics for Prometheus; section: Common Mistakes table
claims.rationale: The official metrics reference documents the exported Hubble metric names, but the docs host is off-allowlist.

claims.claim_text: Unsourced incident framing with a specific date, business type, and seasonal context.
claims.span_hint: section: War Story: The Policy That Ate Christmas
claims.anchor_text: *December 23rd, 2022. Large e-commerce platform. Black Friday went perfectly. Everyone was relaxed.*
claims.suggested_rewrite: *A realistic failure mode: a policy change can accidentally block an overlooked dependency during a busy production period.*
claims.rationale: This is a detailed but unattributed real-world-style incident setup and must lose the fake specifics.

claims.claim_text: Unsourced claim that a junior engineer deployed a policy at 2:47 PM after it worked in staging.
claims.span_hint: section: War Story: The Policy That Ate Christmas
claims.anchor_text: At 2:47 PM, a junior engineer deployed what seemed like a simple change: a new CiliumNetworkPolicy to restrict database access. The policy worked in staging.
claims.suggested_rewrite: A restrictive CiliumNetworkPolicy that looked correct in staging was deployed to production.
claims.rationale: The exact actor and timestamp are unsupported incident specifics.

claims.claim_text: Specific 2:48 PM timestamp in an unsourced incident.
claims.span_hint: section: War Story: The Policy That Ate Christmas
claims.anchor_text: At 2:48 PM:
claims.suggested_rewrite: Soon after deployment:
claims.rationale: Precise timestamps in an unattributed incident should be removed.

claims.claim_text: Specific 2:52 PM timestamp in an unsourced incident.
claims.span_hint: section: War Story: The Policy That Ate Christmas
claims.anchor_text: At 2:52 PM:
claims.suggested_rewrite: A few minutes later:
claims.rationale: Precise timestamps in an unattributed incident should be removed.

claims.claim_text: Specific 2:54 PM timestamp in an unsourced incident.
claims.span_hint: section: War Story: The Policy That Ate Christmas
claims.anchor_text: At 2:54 PM:
claims.suggested_rewrite: When the on-call engineer checked Hubble:
claims.rationale: Precise timestamps in an unattributed incident should be removed.

claims.claim_text: Unsourced claim that root cause was identified in 2 minutes.
claims.span_hint: section: War Story: The Policy That Ate Christmas
claims.anchor_text: - Root cause identified in **2 minutes**
claims.suggested_rewrite: - Root cause identified quickly from the drop verdict and destination
claims.rationale: The exact duration is unsupported false precision.

claims.claim_text: Specific 2:56 PM timestamp in an unsourced incident.
claims.span_hint: section: War Story: The Policy That Ate Christmas
claims.anchor_text: At 2:56 PM:
claims.suggested_rewrite: After the policy was updated:
claims.rationale: Precise timestamps in an unattributed incident should be removed.

claims.claim_text: Unsourced claim that total incident duration was 8 minutes.
claims.span_hint: section: War Story: The Policy That Ate Christmas
claims.anchor_text: **Total incident duration: 8 minutes**
claims.suggested_rewrite: **The outage stayed short because the policy drop was visible quickly.**
claims.rationale: The exact incident duration is unsupported.

claims.claim_text: Unsourced counterfactual that without Hubble the incident would have been a multi-hour outage and teams would have blamed DNS, the load balancer, and the database.
claims.span_hint: section: War Story: The Policy That Ate Christmas
claims.anchor_text: Without Hubble? This would've been a multi-hour outage. The team would've blamed DNS (it's always DNS), then the load balancer, then the database itself. Eventually, maybe, someone would've checked network policies.
claims.suggested_rewrite: Without clear policy visibility, this kind of problem can take much longer to isolate because teams often start by checking other layers first.
claims.rationale: This is unsupported counterfactual incident narration with false precision.

further_reading.title: Cilium Repository README
further_reading.why_relevant: Good high-level reference for Cilium's eBPF dataplane, identity model, L3-L7 policy support, kube-proxy replacement, and observability.

further_reading.title: Hubble Repository README
further_reading.why_relevant: Useful overview of Hubble's observability model, flow visibility, service maps, and security troubleshooting use cases.

further_reading.title: Linux Kernel eBPF Verifier Documentation
further_reading.why_relevant: Primary upstream reference for how the kernel verifier checks eBPF program safety and memory access.

notes: Most Cilium-product specifics in this module are best documented on official docs.cilium.io pages, which are not on the current allowlist; those claims are marked `needs_allowlist_expansion`. Existing inline Datadog/Shopify links are already off-allowlist, and the strongest uncited problems in this module are the two narrative war stories plus the performance comparison table.
